item.title: +1 with gold star
item.content:encoded: Got recommended this and damn was it a good one. Excellent use of death being the lesser of the bad options.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Genuinely not understanding why they don't just destroy the brains? Yeah you can't damage the lattice but the organic matter is still normal. Surely the ethics committee would see the value in ending lives instead of keeping them in perpetual suffering?

EDIT: Ooooooooh never mind it's an End of Death story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is literally my idea of hell. I've been trapped in drug-induced nightmare scenarios that merely felt like an eternity - the thought of it actually BEING an eternity is enough to trauma trigger me.

I guess what I'm saying is your article here is horrifying enough that it it managed to set off my PTSD.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As someone above said, maybe it's a bit too tell-ish when it could use some more show, but it works anyway.

My real question is: why would this be considered an SCP rather than poor random people fucked up by someone with anomalous abilities, and therefore put to death?
It seems like the Foundation knows enough about it to be considered explained, even more considered that is set in End of Death where some magic tech wouldn't be strange at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the grim darkness of the far future, there is only brain herpes.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is fucking depressing. Although you think that the foundation would have constructed some apparatus to inject antidepressants/antipsychotics/feel-good drugs to at least try to alleviate some of the instances situations.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Forget the power of technology and science, for so much has been forgotten, never to be re-learned.
Forget the promise of progress and understanding, for in the grim dark future there is only suffering.
There is no peace amongst the stars, only an eternity of life and consciousness, and the cries of starving gods.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do you want a low-hume reality-bent hellscape? Because that's how ya get one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good at getting a sense of fear and dread out of my cold heart. Well done. I do enjoy End of Death Hub, and the thought of being given the chance to save my concience while leaving my body, only to end up in some kind of robo-sensory purgatory is truly terrifying.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just think of all the SRAs you could make with all those brains! It’s still a little “tell” where it could be “show”, but I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They need to go through and put them all out of their misery with SCP-4514.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: god damn, what a terrifying idea

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, I think this could be more restrained; there's a few word-choices and tonal elements here that I suspect are softening the proverbial 'narrative punch' (by being too overt about what we're supposed to feel).

That being said, it's still excellent execution of a delightfully grimdark idea, and sentences like:

Each brain has been fitted with a lattice of an unknown thaumcially conductive alloy and an electro-thaumic central processing unit, designated SCP-4979-A.

— bid me to upvote.
This might be the best thing I've read all year. Fuuuuuuuuuck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is delectably grimdark.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While reading this, one half of my brain was saying "Wow, this is maybe the darkest thing I've ever read".
The other half was saying "Wow, End of Death is so fucking awesome".
This is a very good thing.

750,000 decaying demented brains / 10

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh jesus christ. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You've made the End of Death canon even more disturbing. Congratulations!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is wonderfully terrifying, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is pretty grisly stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are things worse than the death…

…like this

A very good work

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Absolutely horrifying. The paratech is described both simply enough and in sufficient detail to paint a vivid picture of the attempt at providing a post-body experience and the consequences from its failure. Well done.

+1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
So, you people think my posts are too noblebright, do you? Too happy? Too cartoonishly silly? Well how's this for some grimdarkness: Three-quarters of a million decaying, disembodied brains being tortured in an undying limbo for all eternity? Ah ha ha ha, AH HA HA HA, M'WAH HA HA HA!

+ show block
– hide block

I'm not actually the biggest fan of End of Death, mainly because I've always been interested in futurism and even though much of it is set over a hundred years in the future, End of Death sort of fails at its futurism. The horror of the situation is somewhat contrived, with one transhumanist wiki member even commenting on the rampant 'Deathism1' of the Canon. I spoke with one of the Canon founders on chat, and he had never even heard of Engineered Negligible Senescence. When I criticized the idea that these people would still have American style healthcare, the concept of free, universal healthcare even seemed foreign to him.

The actual rules of the Omega-K don't seem entirely consistent to me either, but I was working off the assumption that any cells responsible for consciousness cannot be fatally damaged, but can receive non-fatal damage that results in declining cognitive functions.

Anyway, despite all of that, I did have an idea for a 'fate worse than death' and thought it would make a good addition to the Canon, so here it is.

This scip is also notable for the reappearance and first official named appearance of The Fixer Uppers, the same parasurgeons responsible for SCP-3619.

*750 000 brains scream in agony*

Ivan: Dmitri, all of our brains have herpes!

Dmitri: *shrugs* these things happen.

Brains: Help us!

Ivan: Fix this!

Dmitri: Okay, let me try something.

*turns of the WiFi. It does not come back on*

Dmitri: Okay, so now we know not to do that.

*sound of Dmitri being dopeslapped*

Long post collapsed. ~Zyn

It looks like this SCP is going to survive, which means I have achieved my random ass goal of having at least one SCP in every century of Series V, just as I did for Series IV. From now until the 5000 contest, any SCPs I post will be posted in the lowest available spot I can get.

Footnotes
1. A term transhumanists use for those who accept death as inevitable and have rationalized it as a good thing.
